Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verb phrase (uku- + verb + object + instrumental phrase); fixed metaphorical construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To boast or speak arrogantly without substance or proof.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, youth discourse, political commentary, humor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian Cham humor where exaggeration was mocked through animal imagery.
First Example Sentence: Ungasikhohlisi, uyadla inja ngomlomo nje kuphela.
Second Example Sentence: Abapolitiki abanengi badla inja ngomlomo ngesikhathi somkhankaso.
Third Example Sentence: UThabo udla inja ngomlomo kodwana akenzi lutho.
